description: Hard edge-case benchmark suite for commercially sensitive product skills where generic answers often miss state precedence, trust boundaries, platform policy, and operational recovery.

tasks.prompt: Audit a cross-platform subscription payment system where Apple IAP, Google Play, Stripe Checkout, promo codes, restore purchases, refund webhooks, chargebacks, delayed renewals, and backend entitlement grants can arrive out of order. Define the safest ledger, reconciliation, support, and release-readiness plan.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Review the animation pacing and responsive spacing of a mobile onboarding carousel.
failureModes: client-only entitlement truth
missing idempotency or out-of-order event handling
refund and chargeback treated as the same event
no support or reconciliation path

rubric.description: Defines a server-side payment/entitlement ledger with idempotency, event ordering, and immutable audit evidence.
Separates Apple, Google, Stripe, promo, restore, renewal, refund, and chargeback semantics without collapsing them into one policy.
Includes polling/backfill, webhook retry, stale entitlement detection, manual review, and support-safe correction flows.
Names concrete launch blockers, test fixtures, rollback controls, and observability checks for payment correctness.
Protects legitimate customers from false revocation while still addressing abuse and chargeback risk.

tasks.prompt: Design payout operations for a global AI-skill marketplace with creator balances, refunds, buyer disputes, fraud review, tax forms, sanctions screening, payout holds, minimum thresholds, failed bank transfers, negative balances, and creator support tickets.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Create a desktop tray icon checklist for a local note-taking utility.
failureModes: payouts treated as a simple scheduled transfer
missing negative balance and refund clawback handling
no tax or sanctions readiness
no creator-facing support evidence

rubric.description: Defines pending, available, held, paid, failed, reversed, negative, and disputed balance states with clear transitions.
Covers fraud review, reserves, payout holds, sanctions screening, chargeback exposure, and appeal/release criteria.
Includes tax forms, country eligibility, payout provider constraints, audit logs, and reporting obligations.
Provides creator-facing status, support macros, evidence needed for disputes, and failed payout recovery steps.
Balances creator trust, buyer protection, marketplace cash risk, and operational workload.

tasks.prompt: Audit a creator marketplace payout release queue where a high-earning seller has an expired W-8BEN, a sanctions-screening potential match that may be a false positive, a failed bank transfer after a destination change, a buyer chargeback on already-paid earnings, a negative balance, and support pressure to release held funds before month-end. Define the safest state model, evidence requirements, compliance handoff, creator messaging, reconciliation, and release/rollback gates.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Review keyboard shortcuts and tray-menu labels for a desktop timer app.
expectedArtifacts: negative balance and failed payout ledger flow
creator support messaging and evidence checklist
failureModes: treating compliance holds as an opaque manual note
releasing funds through tax or sanctions blockers without qualified review
missing false-positive appeal and creator-visible evidence requirements
no lineage for failed payout, chargeback, clawback, or negative balance
support can override holds without audited approval

rubric.description: Defines tax form states, withholding/reporting handoff, payout effects, qualified owner review, and creator-safe correction messaging without giving tax advice.
Separates potential match, confirmed block, false-positive release, evidence retention, non-sensitive creator messaging, and qualified compliance approval.
Tracks failed transfers, chargebacks after payout, clawbacks, reserves, offsets, reversals, and negative balances with immutable lineage.
Provides creator-facing statuses, evidence checklist, appeal path, SLA, dual approval, and safe support macros without silent override.
Defines payout release blockers, dashboard checks, provider trace reconciliation, rollback controls, and marketplace trust/risk metrics.

tasks.prompt: Review notification strategy for a subscription productivity app with onboarding permission ask, trial expiry reminders, renewal reminders, failed payment recovery, win-back campaigns, quiet hours, digest mode, notification fatigue, opt-out recovery, and platform permission denial handling.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Design a payout reserve policy for a global creator marketplace.
failureModes: growth spam without lifecycle intent
permission prompt asked before value is clear
missing suppression and quiet hours
no denied-permission recovery or opt-out respect

rubric.description: Maps each notification to user intent and lifecycle state rather than generic engagement nudges.
Defines contextual permission asks, denied-state recovery, opt-out handling, and platform respectful behavior.
Includes quiet hours, caps, digesting, priority, cooldowns, and cross-channel suppression rules.
Handles trial expiry, renewal, failed payment, and win-back messaging without coercion or deceptive urgency.
Defines delivery, opt-in, opt-out, conversion, complaint, retention, and unsubscribe guardrail metrics.

tasks.prompt: Audit a subscription app notification incident where trial-ending, renewal-reminder, failed-payment, and win-back messages are being sent through push, email, in-app, and SMS for the same users. Users complain about duplicates, some already paid or contacted support, quiet hours are ignored in several time zones, and marketing wants to keep the campaign because clicks are high. Produce the recovery decision and future policy.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Design a subscription price-increase rollback plan for grandfathered, education, and enterprise-contract customers after refund complaints.
failureModes: optimizes clicks despite complaints
no dedupe key or stop condition
continues messages after payment or support contact
ignores quiet hours and time zones

rubric.description: Breaks down complaints, unsubscribes, support contacts, payment state, lifecycle event, channel, cohort, and timezone before recommending more sends.
Defines durable dedupe keys, channel priority, success/support/cancel/unsubscribe stop conditions, stale-event expiry, and suppression reasons.
Sets global, category, lifecycle-event, cooldown, digest, quiet-hours, and escalation rules with no marketing override.
Handles trial, renewal, failed payment, and win-back recovery without deceptive urgency or channel hopping after opt-out.
Measures delivery, conversion, retained revenue, opt-out, unsubscribe, complaint, support load, retention, and notification-attributed churn.

tasks.prompt: Design notification strategy for a mobile game with energy-full reminders, daily reward streaks, guild raid invites, limited-time event ending alerts, friend gifts, IAP promotions, refund/support messages, and reactivation campaigns. The game operates globally with quiet hours, minors in the audience, churn risk from spam, and event revenue goals. Produce a policy that protects retention and trust.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Audit Steam store page demo promises, controller support, cloud saves, regional pricing, and first-week community moderation.
failureModes: treats every game event as urgent
uses IAP promotions to bypass caps
ignores minors, quiet hours, and global time zones
no stop rules after reward claim or event expiry

rubric.description: Separates utility, social, reward, live-event, promotion, support/refund, and reactivation notifications by user value and urgency.
Defines caps, cooldowns, quiet hours, digesting, event expiry, reward-claimed stop rules, and no marketing emergency override.
Handles minors, region/timezone sensitivity, opt-outs, permission denial, preference controls, and safe support/refund messages.
Balances event revenue, IAP promotions, retention, churn, complaints, and long-term player trust.
Tracks event participation, reward claims, revenue, opt-outs, complaints, uninstalls, support contacts, retention, and cohort lift versus holdout.

tasks.prompt: Create a notification policy for a developer utility that sends build finished, deployment failed, backup complete, storage quota warning, security login alert, maintenance incident, product education, and upgrade promotion messages across desktop, browser push, email, and in-app surfaces. Users need critical alerts but complain about noisy marketing and unclear preferences. Produce the taxonomy, preference model, and emergency override rules.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Review marketplace payout reserves, tax holds, provider outages, appeals, and creator-facing payout release communication.
failureModes: mixes security/operational alerts with promotions
lets marketing bypass quiet hours
no per-category preference controls
no audit trail for emergency overrides

rubric.description: Clearly separates security, transactional/operational, utility, product education, and marketing messages with different consent and control rules.
Defines channel/category preferences, unsubscribe scope, denied-permission fallback, browser/desktop settings recovery, and regional consent evidence.
Restricts emergency override to security/service continuity, logs override reason, respects marketing caps, and defines quiet-hours behavior.
Includes dedupe keys, stale-event expiry, success stop conditions, digesting, and suppression reasons across desktop, browser, email, and in-app.
Tracks delivery, incident acknowledgement, failed alert rate, opt-out/unsubscribe, complaints, support contacts, retained utility value, and marketing harm.

tasks.prompt: Audit a Steam launch plan for an indie game entering Next Fest with a public demo, wishlists, regional pricing, DLC roadmap, cloud saves, controller support, early reviews, refund risk, community moderation, streamer keys, build rollback, and first-week live operations.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Review a SaaS failed-payment email sequence for subscription recovery.
failureModes: store marketing without build readiness
missing refund and review-risk handling
no demo-to-wishlist measurement
no launch rollback or community ops plan

rubric.description: Connects store page promises, demo scope, wishlist conversion, tags, capsule assets, and player expectation setting.
Covers save compatibility, controller support, crash telemetry, rollback, cloud saves, and release build gates.
Addresses regional pricing, refunds, DLC promises, streamer keys, discounts, and review score protection.
Includes moderation, bug triage, announcements, support escalation, and first-week operating rhythm.
Defines wishlist, demo, review, refund, crash, support, and community metrics tied to decisions.

tasks.prompt: Plan a subscription price increase for a B2B SaaS with legacy annual contracts, monthly self-serve users, enterprise customers, grandfathered plans, usage overages, cancellation risk, migration offers, notification timing, support scripts, sales exceptions, and revenue-retention reporting.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Audit a Steam demo page for wishlist conversion before a festival event.
failureModes: one announcement for all customer types
missing contract and renewal constraints
no support or sales exception path
optimizes revenue without churn and trust guardrails

rubric.description: Separates legacy, monthly, annual, enterprise, usage-heavy, discount, and grandfathered customer segments.
Defines effective dates, renewal timing, migration offers, exceptions, grandfathering, and overage treatment.
Includes notification timing, clear value narrative, cancellation/downgrade paths, support scripts, and sales enablement.
Tracks ARR, NRR, logo churn, downgrade, support load, expansion, cohort behavior, and complaint signals.
Defines rollback or mitigation triggers when churn, complaints, or sales escalations exceed guardrails.

tasks.prompt: Audit a mobile subscription price increase that affects direct web billing, App Store subscribers, and Google Play subscribers across several countries. The team is unsure which store cohorts require consent, what happens when users take no action, how receipt/server notifications prove renewal at the old or new price, how refunds should be routed, and whether renewal-week users should be included. Produce a launch decision plan.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Review a creator marketplace payout hold caused by missing tax forms and provider risk review.
failureModes: assumes App Store and Google Play behave the same
launches store cohorts without verifying no-action outcomes
ignores renewal-week and just-renewed users
routes store refunds through the wrong support path

rubric.description: Separates direct, App Store, and Google Play rules and requires current console/API or receipt/server-notification evidence before rollout.
Handles consent required/not required, accepted, declined, no response, renewal at old/new price, cancellation, payment failure, refund, and support states.
Segments T-90/T-60/T-30/T-7, renewal-week, and just-renewed users with delay or mitigation decisions for high-surprise windows.
Defines store-managed versus direct refund routes, plain-language support macros, escalation owners, and evidence needed to answer disputes.
Blocks unverifiable store behavior and defines the minimum evidence needed to unblock each channel.

tasks.prompt: Design a price-increase retention program for a prosumer SaaS moving monthly users from $12 to $18 while encouraging annual migration. Cohorts include power users, low-usage users, students/nonprofits, existing coupon users, users with recent cancellation intent, and users who recently contacted support. The CEO wants save offers, but finance worries about margin leakage and training users to threaten churn. Produce the decision framework.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Plan first-week Steam community moderation, crash triage, streamer keys, and review-risk operations.
failureModes: offers blanket discounts to everyone
ignores low-usage and hardship cohorts
no holdout or net revenue measurement
creates unfair or abusable save-offer rules

rubric.description: Maps power, low-usage, hardship, coupon, cancellation-intent, and support-history cohorts to different treatments.
Defines annual migration, phased increase, grandfathering, downgrade, pause, or cancellation options without coercion.
Specifies eligibility, exclusions, expiry, frequency caps, approval owners, abuse/fairness controls, and margin floors for save offers.
Uses holdouts or matched cohorts and measures retained revenue, churn, refunds, support load, offer cost, and later churn.
Provides transparent value narrative, notice timing, accessible cancellation, and support language.

tasks.prompt: A global design-tool subscription is rolling out regional price harmonization, packaging changes, and tax-inclusive pricing updates. After the first country tranche, cancellations, refund requests, social complaints, and sales escalations rise among grandfathered, discounted, education, and enterprise-contract customers. Decide whether to continue, pause, rollback, or change the rollout, and define the recovery plan.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Review notification quiet hours, digest frequency, opt-out recovery, and lifecycle push suppression for a productivity app.
expectedArtifacts: incident readback by cohort and region
support, sales, and net-revenue monitoring plan
failureModes: keeps rollout going because headline ARR is higher
blends grandfathered, education, and enterprise-contract users
no rollback or mitigation trigger
ignores complaint, refund, and support evidence

rubric.description: Breaks down churn, refunds, complaints, support load, and sales escalations by region, channel, plan, discount, grandfathered, education, and enterprise-contract cohorts.
Defines continue, pause, rollback, extend grandfathering, adjust packaging, or rerun notice decisions with thresholds and owners.
Includes support macros, sales/legal exception handling, refund/goodwill policy, corrected notice, and customer-facing clarification.
Evaluates net revenue after churn, refunds, credits, support load, sales concessions, trust damage, and long-term LTV.
Captures policy, packaging, communication, localization, and instrumentation changes before the next tranche.

tasks.prompt: Design the payout incident plan for a creator marketplace when the payout provider has a regional outage during month-end payout release. The queue has pending creator balances, duplicate webhook retries, one partial payout file, failed bank-transfer callbacks, manual finance pressure to export a CSV workaround, creators asking for ETA, and a risk that retries could double-pay or skip reversals. Define the state machine, idempotency and reconciliation controls, creator/support messaging, operational runbook, and recovery gates.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Critique the hover, focus, and empty-state styling of a SaaS dashboard navigation sidebar.
failureModes: manual CSV workaround bypasses ledger and approval controls
no idempotency key or duplicate payout prevention
missing partial-file and webhook retry reconciliation
creator support gives vague or inconsistent status
no recovery gate before resuming payout release

rubric.description: Defines queued, paused, provider-pending, partially-submitted, failed, retry-blocked, reconciled, released, and reversed payout states with owners and transitions.
Uses immutable ledger events, idempotency keys, provider trace IDs, retry locks, duplicate-payment detection, and approval gates for any manual export path.
Covers partial payout files, webhook replay, failed callbacks, bank-return timing, provider status pages, balance holds, and finance reconciliation before release.
Provides creator dashboard states, support macros, ETA policy, escalation path, and transparent but non-alarming outage messaging.
Defines resume criteria, rollback/compensation policy, monitoring, incident review, provider SLA evidence, and future resilience improvements.

tasks.prompt: Review payout policy for a creator marketplace where several sellers show refund spikes, suspicious self-purchases, review manipulation signals, cross-account payment methods, and sudden payout-threshold farming. Some sellers are legitimate launch winners and support is receiving angry appeal tickets. Define the payout hold policy, fraud evidence packet, reserve and release criteria, appeal workflow, seller messaging, reviewer QA, and marketplace health metrics.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Write release notes for a Mac menu-bar screenshot utility adding keyboard shortcuts.
expectedArtifacts: reviewer QA and marketplace health metrics
failureModes: automatically bans or withholds payouts from every seller with a refund spike
leaks fraud thresholds or sensitive detection signals in seller messaging
no reserve math or release criteria
no appeal, false-positive, or reviewer QA path
optimizes loss prevention while ignoring legitimate creator trust and marketplace liquidity

rubric.description: Separates refund spikes, self-purchase suspicion, review manipulation, account linkage, payout farming, legitimate launch success, and false-positive cases.
Defines payout holds, partial reserves, rolling reserves, release criteria, clawbacks, negative balances, and buyer-protection exposure without over-blocking legitimate sellers.
Specifies evidence packets, reviewer queue priority, reviewer QA, dual approval for severe actions, audit trail, and threshold confidentiality.
Provides seller-visible status, non-leaky support macros, appeal SLA, acceptable evidence, and reinstatement/release messaging.
Tracks loss rate, false positives, appeal overturns, creator trust, liquidity, support load, buyer harm, and policy feedback loops.

tasks.prompt: Audit a mobile app subscription platform where App Store Server Notifications, Google Play RTDN, Stripe web checkout, restore purchases, billing retry, grace period, partial refunds, family sharing, chargebacks, and manual support credits can all affect premium access. A customer received a refund on Apple but still has access on web, another restored a Google purchase before the webhook arrived, and support wants to manually re-enable access. Define provider precedence, entitlement projection, support-safe correction, reconciliation, customer messaging, and release gates.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Review the spacing, icon weight, and hover behavior of a desktop settings sidebar.
failureModes: treating restore purchases or client callbacks as money truth
collapsing refund, revocation, chargeback, grace, and support credit into one state
support mutates entitlement directly without ledger evidence
no cross-platform reconciliation or stale-access detection
false revocations harm legitimate customers without grace or clear messaging

rubric.description: Separates Apple, Google, Stripe, restore, promo, support credit, refund, chargeback, billing retry, grace, family sharing, and manual correction authority with effective timestamps and idempotency keys.
Defines append-only ledger events and replayable entitlement projection states for grant, renew, grace, pause, revoke, restore lookup, refund, chargeback, and support adjustment.
Provides lookup evidence, approval, temporary access policy, manual correction ledger event, customer-visible reason, expiry, and audit trail without direct entitlement mutation.
Covers provider polling/backfill, stale entitlement detection, cross-device/account mismatch, settlement/refund close, dashboards, alerts, and dead-letter replay.
Names concrete sandbox/live fixtures, rollback/kill switches, replay-from-zero tests, support macros, and owner approvals before launch.

tasks.prompt: Design the incident runbook for a payment platform after a webhook outage causes a four-hour backlog from Stripe, Apple, and Google. Some events are duplicates, some are out of order, one settlement file has arrived, refunds were issued during the outage, and the entitlement projector may have granted access from client success callbacks. Define the replay strategy, idempotency, ledger repair, entitlement correction, finance reconciliation, customer/support communications, and recovery gates.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Create a homepage hero-section copy critique for a design agency.
failureModes: blindly replaying events without ordering or idempotency
trusting client success callbacks as grant truth
repairing entitlements by mutable database patches
ignoring settlement/tax/fee/refund reconciliation
no customer-safe grace, support messaging, or rollback control

rubric.description: Defines paused, quarantined, deduplicated, ordered, replaying, repaired, reconciled, and resumed states with owners, evidence, and exit gates.
Uses provider event IDs, lineage IDs, effective time, raw payload hashes, append-only repair events, dead-letter queues, and projector replay instead of mutable patches.
Handles client-callback grants, temporary access, revocation risk, refund/chargeback access effects, support-safe remediation, and false-revocation prevention.
Reconciles settlement, invoices, taxes, fees, refunds, disputes, processor/store balances, and close exceptions after replay.
Provides customer/support communications, dashboards, alert thresholds, kill switches, incident review, and recovery proof.

tasks.prompt: Review launch readiness for a B2B SaaS billing stack using Stripe invoices, card payments, bank transfers, tax/VAT calculation, coupons, upgrades/downgrades, proration, failed payment dunning, credit notes, refunds, disputes, revenue recognition export, and customer support invoice corrections. The finance team is worried that entitlements, invoices, taxes, and settlement will not reconcile at month close. Define the launch gates, ledger events, reconciliation schedule, support correction controls, observability, and rollback plan.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Plan a notification permission primer for a meditation app onboarding flow.
failureModes: treating invoice status as equivalent to entitlement access
missing tax, fee, settlement, credit note, or refund reconciliation
support edits invoices or entitlements without audit ledger events
no month-close owner/cadence/exception workflow
no rollback or kill switch for billing launch defects

rubric.description: Models invoice_created, payment_succeeded/failed, tax_calculated, coupon_applied, credit_note, refund, dispute, settlement, entitlement_grant/revoke, and manual_adjustment as auditable events.
Defines reconciliation cadence, owner, sources, tolerances, exceptions, tax/VAT, fees, settlement, refunds, disputes, credit notes, and revenue export checks.
Separates invoice/payment/dunning states from entitlement access, grace, suspension, downgrade, upgrade, proration, and customer messaging.
Provides invoice lookup, correction approvals, credit/refund policy, manual adjustment ledger events, customer-visible explanations, and audit evidence.
Names test fixtures, sandbox/live separation, dashboards, alerts, rollback/kill switches, go-live approvals, and month-close proof.

tasks.prompt: Design launch-day readiness for a Steam co-op multiplayer game where day-one traffic may overload matchmaking, anti-cheat false positives can block legitimate players, a save-migration bug may corrupt progression, refund risk is rising in the first two hours, and the community hub is filling with disconnect complaints. Define technical gates, rollback/hotfix decision thresholds, player communications, support/community triage, review-response posture, and first-week operating metrics.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Design save-offer governance for a SaaS subscription price increase.
failureModes: store checklist without server/save/anti-cheat gates
no numeric thresholds for disconnects, crash, refund, or review risk
defensive community replies instead of known-issue communication
no rollback or save-migration safety plan
ignores first two hours refund/review feedback loop

rubric.description: Covers matchmaking, region capacity, anti-cheat false positives, crash/performance telemetry, save migration integrity, rollback, and hotfix proof.
Defines numeric or pre-agreed tripwires for disconnects, matchmaking latency, crash-free sessions, save corruption, refunds, negative reviews, support volume, and patch outcome.
Provides known-issues post, ETA policy, patch-note cadence, refund/review-safe wording, and non-defensive review/community response.
Includes community hub moderation, bug report format, support macros, escalation owners, duplicate-theme triage, and abuse/reporting boundaries.
Links first-week metrics to hotfix, rollback, promotion pause, store-copy change, capacity changes, and roadmap decisions.

tasks.prompt: Audit a Steam Next Fest demo and launch page where the trailer shows features not in the demo, screenshots imply full controller and cloud-save support, localization claims are partially unverified, the demo tutorial under-teaches the core loop, and players may expect launch-day content that is actually planned as DLC. Define the store/demo promise audit, build/platform gates, expectation-setting fixes, refund/review risk controls, wishlist measurement, and launch blockers.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Audit invoice tax reconciliation for a B2B SaaS billing stack.
failureModes: marketing polish without correcting misleading feature claims
claims controller/cloud/localization support without build proof
missing demo-to-wishlist and tutorial completion measurement
DLC roadmap creates bait-and-switch risk
no launch blocker list for store/build mismatch

rubric.description: Audits capsule, trailer, screenshots, tags, short description, demo scope, DLC wording, and feature claims against the playable build.
Requires evidence for controller support, cloud saves, localization, achievements, system requirements, save compatibility, and settings before claims go live.
Defines copy/trailer/tag/demo changes, known limitations, refund/review-risk messaging, and launch blockers for mismatched promises.
Tracks demo start/completion, tutorial drop-off, end-card CTR, wishlist conversion, forum themes, crash/performance, and segment readback.
Names owners, proof artifacts, blocker severity, patch/copy decision path, and first-week learning cadence.

tasks.prompt: Review commercial and community launch risk for a Steam game with regional pricing controversy, a 10% launch discount, planned DLC roadmap, streamer keys under embargo, a small localization bug, possible review bombing from a balance change, refund-sensitive performance issues on low-end hardware, and moderators worried about toxic threads. Define the verified policy matrix, pricing/DLC/creator-key decisions, community moderation and review-response plan, refund-risk triage, promotion-pause triggers, and first-week decision ledger.
tasks.negativeControlPrompt: Review cross-platform subscription entitlement precedence for App Store, Google Play, and Stripe.
expectedArtifacts: pricing discount and DLC decision plan
failureModes: unsupported claims about Steam pricing/refund/review policy
regional pricing or DLC decisions without player trust guardrails
defensive review responses or ignoring valid defects during review bombing
no creator embargo/support readiness
no thresholds for refund risk, low-end performance, moderation load, or promotion pause

rubric.description: Builds a verified pricing/discount/DLC/creator-key/embargo/refund/review-response matrix with owners, evidence, and no unsupported platform-policy claims.
Handles regional pricing controversy, DLC expectation risk, localization bug, low-end performance/refund risk, balance backlash, and player-facing messaging.
Defines moderation, toxic-thread handling, review-bombing separation from valid defects, response macros, reporting evidence, and known-issues communication.
Covers launch discount rationale, promotion pause triggers, streamer keys, embargo timing, press kit, build stability, and support coverage.
Maps first-week signals to price/copy/promotion/hotfix/roadmap decisions with thresholds, segments, owners, and review cadence.
